description: Get a single activity link by its id

id.description: The unique identifier for the activity link

from_activity.description: The ID of the predecessor activity

to_activity.description: The ID of the successor activity

relationship_type.description: The type of dependency relationship (SS, SF, FS, FF)

lag.description: The time delay between the activities

lag_unit.description: The unit of time for the lag

lag_display_unit.description: The unit in which lag is displayed in the UI

active.description: Indicates whether the dependency is active

created_at.description: The date and time the link was created (ISO 8601 format)

created_by.description: The ID of the user who created the link

updated_at.description: The date and time the link was updated (ISO 8601 format)

updated_by.description: The ID of the user who updated the link

400.description: The server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax or parameters that fail to meet validation requirements.

401.description: Although the HTTP standard specifies "unauthorized", semantically this response means "unauthenticated". That is, the client must authenticate itself to get the requested response.

403.description: The client does not have access rights to the content; that is, it is authenticated, but the server is refusing to give the requested resource. Unlike 401, the client's identity is known to the server.

404.description: The server can not find the requested resource. This can mean either the URL is not recognized, or the resource itself does not exist. Servers may also send this response instead of 403 to hide the existence of a resource from an unauthorized client.
